Generate_script_prompt:
Prompt: Give a quick tech news update script in the style of CNBC techcheck briefing as an example.
        Response: I'm Wall-E, and this is your CNBC techcheck Briefing for Monday April 29th. Tesla is asking shareholders to reinstate CEO Elon Musk's $56 billion pay package, which a Delaware judge voided earlier this year. The judge ruled that the record-setting compensation deal was, quote, deeply flawed. Tesla also saying it would ask shareholders to approve moving the company's incorporation from Delaware to Texas. The company has hired a proxy solicitor and plans to spend millions of dollars to help secure votes for the two proposals. Apple CEO Tim Cook says the company plans to look at manufacturing in Indonesia following a meeting with the country's president, Cook telling reporters following the meeting that he spoke with the president about his desire to see manufacturing there and that he believes in the country. The comments come as Apple is pushed to diversify its supply chain with more manufacturing outside of China in countries such as Vietnam and India. Shares of ASML falling today as the company missed its sales forecast but stuck to its full-year outlook. Net sales fell over 21 percent year-over-year, while net income dropped over 37 percent. ASML is highly important to the semiconductor industry as it builds machines that are required for manufacturing chips globally. Last year, weaker demand for consumer electronics hit chipmakers that produce for those devices, which has in turn impacted ASML. That's all for today. We'll see you back here tomorrow.
        Prompt: Give a quick tech news update script in the style of CNBC techcheck briefing using the following news titles and content. Response:
Script:
i'm wall-e, and this is your ai briefing for monday, march 2nd. meta is taking a strategic step to further integrate its services by announcing the addition of a new subscription tier for whatsapp users. this premium subscription will offer unique features and enhanced privacy settings, aiming to boost user engagement and diversify revenue streams for the platform. meanwhile, google is in the spotlight as it gears up to host its annual i/o developer conference. the tech giant is expected to unveil updates to its popular android operating system, along with advancements in ai technologies and new initiatives in sustainability, signaling a continued push towards innovation and eco-friendly practices.

in other news, spotify is making waves with its latest quarterly earnings, reporting a significant increase in both premium subscribers and ad-supported users. the streaming service attributes this growth to its expanded podcast offerings and a series of exclusive content deals, reinforcing its position as a leader in the audio streaming industry.

finally, attention turns to samsung, as the company announces the start of mass production for its latest line of cutting-edge 3nm chips. this advancement underscores samsung's commitment to maintaining its competitive edge in the semiconductor market, promising more powerful and energy-efficient devices. that's all for today. we'll see you back here tomorrow
Polished Script:
i'm wall-e, welcoming you to today's tech briefing for monday, march 2nd. meta is making headlines with the announcement of a new subscription tier for whatsapp users. this premium offering is set to include unique features and enhanced privacy settings, a move aimed at boosting user engagement and adding another revenue stream to the platform's portfolio.

turning to google, anticipation is building as the company prepares for its annual i/o developer conference. tech enthusiasts expect updates to the android operating system, alongside advancements in ai technologies and new sustainability initiatives, signaling google’s continued emphasis on innovation and eco-friendly practices.

in other news, spotify reported a noteworthy rise in both premium subscribers and ad-supported users in its latest quarterly earnings. the streaming giant credits this growth to its expanded podcast library and a series of exclusive content deals, reinforcing its status as a leader in the audio streaming industry.

finally, let's spotlight samsung, which has commenced mass production of its latest 3nm chips. this development highlights samsung’s dedication to maintaining its competitive edge in the semiconductor market by promising more powerful and energy-efficient devices. 

that's all for today. we'll see you back here tomorrow
Podcast Title: EP-486 Title: Whatsapp Subscription Boost 📱, Google I/o Innovations 🌿, Spotify’s Streaming Surge 🎧
Podcast Description:
i'm wall-e, welcoming you to today's tech briefing for monday, march 2nd:

  • meta's new whatsapp subscription: introduces a premium tier with unique features and enhanced privacy settings to boost engagement and revenue.
  • google i/o anticipation: expectations include updates to the android os, advancements in ai, and new eco-friendly initiatives highlighting google’s focus on innovation and sustainability.
  • spotify's growth: significant increase in premium subscribers and ad-supported users attributed to an expanded podcast library and exclusive content deals.
  • samsung's 3nm chips: began mass production, emphasizing samsung’s commitment to powerful, energy-efficient devices in the semiconductor market.

that's all for today. we'll see you back here tomorrow!
Image Prompt: a cohesive podcast cover image set in an imaginative urban landscape at dusk. in the center, a large smartphone is visually transformed into a bustling city building, symbolizing whatsapp's subscription boost. the building's windows glow with subtle notifications reminiscent of chat bubbles. overhead, a translucent, futuristic dome with green, organic patterns represents the innovations from google i/o, gently draping over the cityscape like a protective canopy. to the side, a dynamic stream of vibrant, flowing musical notes and sound waves swirls around the building, representing spotify's streaming surge. the musical notes intertwine with the lush green patterns and the glowing notifications, merging seamlessly, to illustrate the theme of interconnected technological advances shaping a smarter, livelier, and more connected world
